title: Concrete Pour Thermal Maturity Controller
description: Closed-loop heating-blanket controller that monitors four embedded PT100 probes in fresh concrete, enforces hydration thermal-gradient limits, computes the Nurse-Saul maturity index in real time, and logs all data to SD card for structural compliance reporting.
objective: Hold cure temperature at 65 °C setpoint, limit cross-section gradient to ≤15 °C, accumulate maturity index every second, trigger safe shutdown on overtemperature or excessive gradient.
constraints: Arduino Uno; 12 V 10 A blanket; four embedded PT100s; SD card logging; hardware fault shutdown; BOM < $40.
supporting_fact_titles: Concrete Hydration Exotherm and Thermal Gradient Limits
Maturity Method for Concrete Compressive Strength Estimation
element_usage_notes: Four probes at pour corners and centre-top; gradient = max − min channel
Duty cycle set by PID output 0-255; hardware OCP via MOSFET Rdson
CSV row: epoch, T0-T3, avg, gradient, pwm, maturity, fault
Provides epoch seconds for each log row for compliance traceability

element_interactions.description: PID controller uses average RTD reading as process variable to set blanket PWM duty
All channel values written to CSV each second
RTC supplies absolute timestamp for each log row
Actuator state co-logged with sensor data for audit trail
